content:
Self-supervised learning emerged as researchers sought to **exploit structure inside unlabeled data** when human labels were too scarce or expensive.

Early **word embedding** work (word2vec, 2013) showed that predicting context words from a center word — a self-supervised objective — learns useful semantic vectors without explicit sentiment or topic labels.

**Autoencoding** and **denoising autoencoders** reconstructed inputs from corrupted versions, foreshadowing modern masked modeling.

In computer vision, **rotation prediction** (Gidaris et al., 2018) and **jigsaw puzzles** demonstrated that surrogate classification tasks on unlabeled images yield transferable convolutional features.

**Contrastive learning** (SimCLR, MoCo, 2020) scaled SSL by pulling together augmented views of the same instance and pushing apart different instances.

In NLP, **BERT** (2018) popularized **masked language modeling** on large corpora, while **GPT** (2018 onward) scaled **causal next-token prediction** — both self-supervised pretraining recipes that underpin today's **generative AI** stack.

**CLIP** (2021) extended contrastive self-supervision to image–text pairs from the web, aligning multimodal representations without task-specific human labels at pretrain time.

timeline.label: word2vec (2013)
timeline.description: Skip-gram and CBOW predict context from word or word from context — early large-scale self-supervision for text embeddings.

timeline.label: Rotation / jigsaw SSL (2018)
timeline.description: Vision community formalizes pretext classification tasks on unlabeled ImageNet-scale data.

timeline.label: BERT & GPT (2018–2019)
timeline.description: Transformer pretraining via masked LM and next-token prediction becomes the dominant NLP recipe.

timeline.label: SimCLR / MoCo (2020)
timeline.description: Contrastive self-supervision reaches competitive ImageNet transfer with linear probes.

timeline.label: CLIP & LLM scale (2021+)
timeline.description: Multimodal contrastive training and trillion-token causal LM pretraining drive modern foundation models.
